mnemonics.title: IGADV Process
mnemonics.content: Investigate Stakeholders → Gather Information → Analyze Threats → Document Requirements → Validate (IGADV) - The elicitation workflow

mnemonics.title: AADI Requirements
mnemonics.content: Authentication, Authorization, Data Protection, Input Validation - Core security requirement categories

mnemonics.title: ACTS Techniques
mnemonics.content: Abuse Cases, Threat Modeling, Security Use Cases, Stakeholder Interviews - Key elicitation methods

mnemonics.title: CIA Triad
mnemonics.content: Confidentiality (C), Integrity (I), Availability (A) - The security golden triangle

acronyms.usage: Core security objectives to validate requirements against

acronyms.expansion: Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information Disclosure, Denial of Service, Elevation of Privilege
acronyms.usage: Threat categories to check during threat modeling
Overall process of integrating security into SDLC

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a castle with multiple gates (entry points), guards (controls), and wanted posters (known threats) on the walls
Picture red attack arrows flowing through a system diagram, highlighting vulnerable pathways
Visualize a treasure chest with multiple locks (encryption layers) sinking in the ocean (secure transmission)
See a factory conveyor belt with filters catching malformed packages (invalid inputs)

analogies.analogy: Like building earthquake-resistant foundations - defines structural integrity before construction starts
Similar to a military war game - simulate attacks to identify defense weaknesses
Fire drills for software - predefined responses to emergency scenarios
Like a diplomatic pouch - sealed, tracked, and accessible only to authorized handlers
